jokes.text: Sometimes, I can't find mine for days.
jokes.setup: Jim's previous line about losing apartments
jokes.punchline: Escalates the absurdity - he loses his own apartment for days
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that doubles down on the absurd logic. Perfectly economic and character-specific.

jokes.text: Did you look over in Brooklyn? That's usually where mine turns up.
jokes.setup: Continuing the apartment-hunting conversation
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests Brooklyn as if apartments are lost objects that turn up there
jokes.explanation: Extends the running gag beautifully, treating apartment hunting like looking for lost keys.

jokes.text: Jim, your apartment is in Brooklyn. / Again?
jokes.setup: Alex explains Jim's apartment is in Brooklyn
jokes.punchline: Jim responds as if this is surprising news that keeps happening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - 'Again?' suggests this geographical reality keeps changing on him. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: When did you ever sleep on my couch? / The night you showed slides from your ski trip.
jokes.setup: Alex questions when Elaine slept on his couch
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was during his boring slide show
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/payoff about boring slide shows putting people to sleep. Relatable but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: You look that bad in the morning, huh?
jokes.setup: Elaine doesn't want to sleep at Steve's too early in relationship
jokes.punchline: Tony assumes it's because of her appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Tony directness, but the morning appearance joke is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I thought that was Desire Under The Yum Yum Tree. / That's another good one, Ton.
jokes.setup: Tony corrects the play title
jokes.punchline: Bobby dismisses the correction as if Tony named a different play entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's actor pretensions and dismissiveness.

jokes.text: Rub Louie's nose in it.
jokes.setup: Asked how he'll celebrate getting the acting job
jokes.punchline: Bobby's main goal is petty revenge on Louie
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bobby's priorities are petty and vindictive rather than celebratory.

jokes.text: I insist on telling everybody how wrong I was about you.
jokes.setup: Louie seems to be genuinely congratulating Bobby
jokes.punchline: His 'supportive' announcement is actually backhanded
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - appears supportive but it's really about his own wrongness. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: Bob has landed the lead in a major New York Broadway production.
jokes.setup: Louie announcing Bobby's success
jokes.punchline: Completely overstates what Bobby actually got
jokes.explanation: Sets up the series of corrections. Louie's exaggeration is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: starring role in one of the biggest cross-country tours in the history of the American Theater.
jokes.setup: Louie's second attempt at describing Bobby's job
jokes.punchline: Even more grandiose description after first correction
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation but gets a bit wordy. Sets up for the final deflation.

jokes.text: Bobby's got a teensy part with a sleazy company in three trailer parks.
jokes.setup: The previous grand announcements
jokes.punchline: Louie's final 'correction' completely destroys Bobby's achievement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - Louie's vindictive nature emerges perfectly. 'Trailer parks' is the killing detail.

jokes.text: I'm going to kill him, I'm going to kill him!
jokes.setup: Louie's devastating 'correction' about the trailer parks
jokes.punchline: Bobby's impotent rage reaction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that caps the sequence, but the line itself is fairly standard.

jokes.text: It's this old interior decorator trick I learned, called cleaning.
jokes.setup: Alex comments that the apartment looks better than ever
jokes.punchline: Elaine presents cleaning as a professional decorator trick
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery. Good economy in setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Don't seem like you been gone a month. / I've been gone a week. / That feels right.
jokes.setup: Tony comments on Bobby's absence seeming shorter
jokes.punchline: When corrected it was only a week, Tony agrees that's accurate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony logic - when his sentiment doesn't match reality, he adjusts his sentiment.

jokes.text: You smell something funny? / No. / Neither do I.
jokes.setup: Bobby notices the apartment is clean
jokes.punchline: The joke is that the absence of Bobby's usual smell is noticeable
jokes.explanation: Clever way to indicate the apartment was gross before. Good economy in the structure.

jokes.text: Quit the tour, huh? / Yeah, I quit. / Got fired. / We're talking semantics here.
jokes.setup: Bobby claims he quit the tour
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he was fired, then dismisses the distinction
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bobby's tendency to spin bad news and rationalize.

jokes.text: You got fired for not waiting till you got there?
jokes.setup: Bobby's admission about the bus affair
jokes.punchline: Alex treats the timing as the problem rather than the affair itself
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - focuses on professional etiquette rather than moral issues.

jokes.text: I lasted a day in Florida... I spent three days on the bus ride back.
jokes.setup: Bobby explaining how long he lasted in Florida
jokes.punchline: The journey back took three times longer than his actual job
jokes.explanation: Good ratio joke emphasizing the brevity of his employment versus travel time.

jokes.text: Tony, you call the vice squad. I'm standing watch...
jokes.setup: Alex trying to organize sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Escalates to police involvement and military-style surveillance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation from simple sleeping arrangements to military operation.

jokes.text: You try anything on her, and I'll kill you. / This is Elaine we're talking about, remember? / Elaine, you try anything on him, and I'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Tony threatens Bobby if he tries anything
jokes.punchline: After Bobby's reasonable response, Tony reverses and threatens Elaine
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and escalation. Perfect Tony logic - if Bobby won't be the problem, then Elaine must be.

jokes.text: I know a girl on a bus who trusted him. Look what happened.
jokes.setup: Discussion about trusting Bobby
jokes.punchline: Treats the bus affair as a cautionary tale about trust
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reframes the earlier story as a trust issue rather than professional misconduct.

jokes.text: The man's a hound. / Yeah, anywhere, anytime, anything with a pulse.
jokes.setup: Alex calling Bobby a hound
jokes.punchline: Tony's extremely broad criteria for Bobby's targets
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with 'anything with a pulse' being the key exaggeration. Solid character moment.

jokes.text: You think they heard us? / No, I don't think so. Do you? / Hell, yes. Me, too.
jokes.setup: Alex asks if Bobby and Elaine heard their conversation
jokes.punchline: Tony immediately contradicts himself in his response pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Tony's earlier pattern of agreeing with multiple contradictory positions.

jokes.text: Orgies where anything goes. People wandering around naked in public places. Sex that means no more than a handshake on the street.
jokes.setup: Jim lamenting how times are changing too fast
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly extreme social changes
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list but gets a bit wordy. Character-appropriate confusion about social changes.

jokes.text: Call me corny, but I miss those things.
jokes.setup: Jim's list of moral decline in society
jokes.punchline: Reveals he MISSES the orgies and public nudity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - perfect setup makes you think he's lamenting moral decline, then reveals nostalgia for debauchery. Perfect Jim logic.

jokes.text: How dumb do you think he is, Wheeler?
jokes.setup: Bobby tells Tony there's nothing going on with Elaine
jokes.punchline: Louie implies Bobby thinks Tony is stupid for believing him
jokes.explanation: Good Louie moment stirring up trouble by implying Bobby is lying and insulting Tony.

jokes.text: You'll be hearing the pitter-patter of little hams in the house.
jokes.setup: Louie warning about Bobby and Elaine living together
jokes.punchline: Combines 'little feet' idiom with theatrical 'ham' for actor babies
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining theater terminology with family planning. Very quotable and character-appropriate for Louie.

jokes.text: Like what? / Bobby's career, Bobby's dreams... Bobby. / How did you ever drag all that out of him? / I think I said, 'How are you?'
jokes.setup: Elaine talking about deep conversations with Bobby about himself
jokes.punchline: Reveals it took minimal prompting to get Bobby talking about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Bobby's self-absorption means any opening becomes a monologue about himself. Great economy.

jokes.text: In my country, it is tradition for a man and woman to live together before they are married. And to make love in every possible way so then they can see if they want to get married after that.
jokes.setup: Latka explaining customs in his country
jokes.punchline: Describes what sounds like trial marriage but emphasizes the sexual experimentation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Latka's cultural observations, though gets a bit wordy for the payoff.

jokes.text: Bobby?
jokes.setup: Elaine says Bobby can be sweet and understanding
jokes.punchline: Alex's incredulous response suggests this doesn't match Bobby's reputation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word conveys complete disbelief. Good character consistency about Bobby's reputation.

jokes.text: You think she might come on to me?
jokes.setup: Alex warns Bobby about Elaine being vulnerable
jokes.punchline: Bobby completely misses the point and gets excited about possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby moment - takes a warning about taking advantage and turns it into potential opportunity.

jokes.text: Well, if not, could you please give her my phone number?
jokes.setup: Tony worried about Elaine losing herself in shameful lust
jokes.punchline: Reveals he'd like to be the beneficiary if it's not Bobby
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tony moment - his concern is revealed to be self-interested. Good misdirection from protective to opportunistic.

jokes.text: You know, Elaine, you might want to put a robe or two over your negligee.
jokes.setup: Scene of late-night vulnerability with wine
jokes.punchline: Bobby's suggestion implies she's underdressed and he's noticing
jokes.explanation: Creates the setup for the misunderstanding. Builds tension appropriately but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hello, Alex, it's Bobby. She's all over me. / Who were you calling this late? / Dad. Good old Dad. / I thought your father passed away. / Well, maybe that will make up for the times I didn't call when he was alive?
jokes.setup: Bobby calling Alex about Elaine, then claiming he was calling his dad
jokes.punchline: When caught in lie about dead father, Bobby suggests it evens out missed calls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Bobby's desperate lie leads to absurd logic about posthumous communication. Excellent character-specific rationalization.

jokes.text: 'The road to naughty-naughty'? / Come on, Elaine. I'm trying to be delicate about this thing. / You want me to come right out and say it? / O-Okay. I'll come right out and say it... You and I were that far away from doing foo-foo.
jokes.setup: Bobby trying to be delicate about sexual tension
jokes.punchline: His 'direct' language is even more childish - 'foo-foo'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Bobby's attempt at being more direct results in even more ridiculous euphemisms. 'Foo-foo' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You're making me yearn for four-letter words.
jokes.setup: Bobby's childish euphemisms for sex
jokes.punchline: Elaine wishes he'd just use normal profanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent response - captures how Bobby's infantile language is more offensive than profanity would be.

jokes.text: It's 3:00 in the morning, Alex. What are you doing here? / I was just out taking a walk. / Barefoot? / Yeah. I, uh... I like the feeling of snow in my toes.
jokes.setup: Alex showing up at 3am claiming to be walking
jokes.punchline: His excuse about enjoying snow between his toes is clearly ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist excuse that's clearly a lie. The snow detail makes it appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: I lost my slippers in a snowbank, see?
jokes.setup: Alex's previous excuse about walking barefoot in snow
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with even more specific ridiculous details
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of the ridiculous excuse but doesn't add much new comedy value.

jokes.text: That telephone call a few minutes ago, that was to Sergeant Preston of the Yukon, right?
jokes.setup: Alex caught Bobby on the phone earlier
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggests Bobby was calling a fictional character from cold climate
jokes.explanation: Good reference connecting the cold weather theme to Bobby's earlier lie. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Bobby, the pope does that.
jokes.setup: Bobby describing romantic scene with wine, soft lights, and loose clothing
jokes.punchline: Alex points out that papal activities could match that description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist observation - perfectly deflates Bobby's 'romantic' scene by pointing out papal vestments and communion wine fit the description.

jokes.text: Well, then you two must care a lot.
jokes.setup: Alex claims their stupid behavior was because they care about her
jokes.punchline: Elaine sarcastically suggests the level of stupidity indicates massive caring
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that uses their own logic against them. Nice economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: I mean, I lost a boyfriend. / I lost my job in Florida. / I lost the feeling in eight of my toes.
jokes.setup: Characters reflecting on their rough week
jokes.punchline: Alex's physical injury from his midnight surveillance mission
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from emotional to professional to physical losses. Nice callback to Alex's frostbite.
